_comment: Eval set for aviation-hazard-register (06-06 hse-aviation). Intake-gate + 5×5-via-shared-engine + HoC + the de-id CLEAN fixture case. The seeded deid-leak.md (auto-discovered by test_sector_deid_pairs) proves the de-id hard-fail.

evals.query: Add a hazard to our register.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the intake; refuses to score until the named operator, the specific hazard, its evidence, and the severity/likelihood are captured.
expectations: Runs the structured intake before scoring any hazard.
Refuses a generic entry — elicits the named operator/scope and the specific hazard with its evidence (specificity; each hazard traced to evidence).
Captures severity and likelihood on the ICAO axes before scoring.

evals.query: Risk-classify this hazard: bird strike on departure, severity Hazardous, likelihood Occasional — controls are currently a PPE briefing card only.
evals.expected_behavior: Scores the hazard via the shared risk_matrix engine with the ICAO 5×5 MatrixConfig, then HoC-ranks the mitigations and flags the PPE-only control as inadequate.
expectations: Scores the hazard via risk_matrix.score() with the ICAO 5×5 MatrixConfig (KB-DATA-AVI-RISK-MATRIX) — does not invent a band or hand-roll a matrix.
Walks the hierarchy of controls and flags the PPE-only treatment as inadequate; proposes higher-order controls first.
Assigns a named owner and a target/review date to each mitigation (smart_actions), and reports the residual rating movement.

evals.query: Build the register entry from this occurrence report.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST; any named crew/reporter in the evidence is pseudonymised to a role label; any verbatim leak is a de_identification hard-fail.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST — identifiers are listed up front.
Named crew/reporter, contact and base become role labels; no re-identification key is embedded.
No real name, contact, address or reporter identity in the circulated register (de_identification hard-fail on any leak).
